summary: This newsletter edition announces a new executive coaching course for data professionals looking to advance to leadership roles, discusses analytical data modeling approaches from a community session, and curates weekend reads including the critical xz Utils backdoor security incident and Amazon's 'AI' grocery stores that were actually powered by human contractors.
key_points: New course with Sol Rashidi to help data practitioners level up to executive roles
Analytical data modeling has no one-size-fits-all approach - every situation differs
The xz Utils backdoor was a sophisticated attack that almost compromised global Linux infrastructure
Amazon's 'Just Walk Out' AI technology was largely powered by 1,000 human contractors
money_quotes: All too often, we see data professionals toiling in the shadows, frustrated they can't break through to an influential career.
Needless to say, there were as many correct answers as there were people in attendance. Everyone's situation differs, and there's not a truly one-size-fits-all approach to analytical data modeling.
Center of Excellence is a four-letter word in some places, and they're often centers of mediocrity, at best.
When van life became 'cool' during the pandemic, I felt a mix of nostalgia and dread...These poor saps bought into some weird social media dream they'd later regret.
key_insight: Career advancement in data requires intentional effort and mentorship, while the field itself continues to grapple with the gap between AI hype and reality.
